commit.message:
Update install instructions to use PyPI package

Now that efn-mcp is published on PyPI, simplify install to just
'uvx --prerelease=allow efn-mcp' instead of git+https.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: per-flavor platform override + BTRFS disk pressure on GDX builds

build-variant.yml: support per-flavor platforms field in build-config.yml
(.platforms // $v.platforms) so flavors can override the variant default.

build-config.yml: restrict albacore gnome50/gnome50-hwe/gnome50-gdx to
x86_64 only. libinput >= 1.27.0 (required by mutter-50) has only been
backported to x86_64 in the COPR. arm64 excluded until aarch64 libinput
lands (https://github.com/tuna-os/github-copr/issues/18).

Justfile: also remove the chain base image from BTRFS storage before
podman load. For GDX (S3+) builds the pulled S2 chain base image stays
in BTRFS alongside the pre-chunk image, pushing total usage high enough
to trigger the podman storage index corruption bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tunaOS
repository.description: Bringing a modern cloud-native experience to the Enterprise Linux Desktop

commit.message:
fix: honor launch port and stabilize stream

Propagate launched CDP ports across later connections, rotate cached clients when the resolved endpoint changes, and preserve requested-symbol settling in bounded price streams without repeated symbol switching.

Also surface unsuccessful stream results as MCP errors and keep launch port rollback behavior safe when a stored session port fails to connect.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v0.3.1: cross-file rename, refs precision, reindex command

- rename: reads disk files for closed buffers so all files get renamed
- rename: use rg_find_references + rename_in_scope for cross-file edits
- rename: scoped by parent class / package (same logic as references)
- refs: scoped rg search (Pass A qualified + Pass B import-gated bare)
- refs: decl_files filtered by enclosing_class_at to prevent bleed-across
- refs: wrapped in spawn_blocking to avoid blocking Tokio runtime
- refs: import/package lines filtered from rg results
- refs: in-memory supplement scoped to current file only
- reindex: workspace command 'kotlin-lsp/reindex' for full re-parse
- indexer: refactored index_workspace into index_workspace_impl
- tests: 3 regression tests for refs precision (187 total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Performance Metrics endpoint

 PerformanceReport

Types (browser.ts):
-  full NavTiming L1 breakdown (dns, connect, ttfb, domLoad, etc.)NavigationTiming
-  LCP, FCP, CLS, INP, TTFB (all nullable ms/score)CoreWebVitals
-  name, type, duration, transferSizeResourceTimingEntry
-  envelope wrapping vitals + navigation + slowResources + rawMetricsPerformanceReport

Implementation (tab-manager.ts):
- capturePerformanceMetrics(): CDP Performance.enable/getMetrics for raw counters;
  Runtime.evaluate queries window.performance.timing (NavTiming L1) and
  getEntriesByType() for LCP, FCP, CLS (summed non-input-gated shifts),
  INP (longest event entry); top-20 slowest resources by duration

REST (agent-server.ts):
- GET /api/tabs/:id/performance

SDK (agent-sdk/src/index.ts):
- getPerformanceMetrics(tabId): Promise<PerformanceReport>
- PerformanceReport re-exported

Docs (docs/dev-team-features.md):
- Section 4 updated with full API docs, usage examples, response shape,
  implementation notes, and CDP domain table row

commit.message:
feat: Cross-platform CLI configurator for cloud-init generation (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: add cross-platform CLI configurator for cloud-init generation

Adds an interactive Python CLI tool (dev-server-provision/configurator/)
that guides users through generating cloud-init.yaml files with:
- Multi-provider support (Hetzner, AWS, GCP, Azure, DigitalOcean)
- Domain, DNS, and Cloudflare configuration
- AI agent setup with API key entry (Copilot, Claude, Gemini, OpenCode)
- Input validation and preflight checks
- cloud-init YAML generation matching existing template
- Optional Hetzner CLI deployment execution
- 74 unit tests for validators, generator, and providers

feat(scan): auto-scan on window focus + optimize scan performance

- Add window-focus-in hook to auto-run 'pilot scan' silently
- Run scan before restore on tmux server start
- Optimize: use libc::kill for PID checks (no subprocess spawn)
- Optimize: only walk process trees for panes running copilot/node
- Quiet output for hook usage (stderr only when matches found)

This makes session links self-correcting: if user manually
launches copilot --resume in a window, the next focus switch
will detect and update the mapping automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(zen): explicitly enable all Firefox Sync engines in user.js

Ensures addons, bookmarks, history, passwords, prefs, and tabs sync
engines are on so they can't be accidentally disabled. The browser
still needs to be opened once after signedInUser.json is written to
trigger the first sync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[aspnetcore] Make crypto algorithms configurable for DataProtection (#985)

* [aspnetcore] Make crypto algorithms configurable for DataProtection

Added CryptographicAlgorithms property to AzureDataProtectionOptions, allowing selection of encryption and validation algorithms (defaulting to AES_256_GCM and HMACSHA512). Updated ServiceCollectionExtensions to use this property. Bumped version to 8.43.0. Centralizes and increases flexibility of cryptographic configuration.

* Apply suggestions from code review

commit.message:
Upgrade Sybase ASE connector to multi-table dynamic discovery (#542)

* Upgrade Sybase ASE connector to multi-table dynamic discovery

- Replaced single-table (sales only) connector with full multi-table connector
- Dynamically discovers and syncs all tables in the configured database
- Added primary key detection via sp_helpindex stored procedure
- Added dynamic Fivetran schema mapping for all Sybase ASE data types
- Fixed DECIMAL type conversion (Decimal -> str) for Fivetran protobuf compatibility
- Added incremental sync support with per-table state tracking
- Added optional table filtering via 'tables' config field
- Added sybase_ase_load_test.py for performance and regression testing
- Updated README with full setup, configuration, and usage instructions

* Fix flake8 linting errors in sybase_ase connector

- Remove trailing whitespace from SQL query string
- Remove unused variable col_length
- Flatten multi-line boolean condition to avoid W503
- Add missing whitespace around / operator in f-strings (E226)

* Fix black/flake8 conflict on incremental column condition

Rewrote multi-condition if statement to avoid W503 (flake8) while
also satisfying black's line-length formatting requirements

* Remove safe_log wrapper - use Fivetran SDK log directly

Per code review feedback: safe_log() was a defensive workaround for
standalone testing, but is unnecessary since connector.debug() properly
initializes the SDK runtime and logging. Replaced all safe_log() calls
with log.info/warning/severe directly, removed the fallback to Python
standard logging, and removed the unused logging import.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rewrite README for multi-table connector

- Removed all references to single-table sales-only connector
- Removed references to deleted HammerDB workload scripts and docs
- Documented dynamic schema discovery (sysobjects, syscolumns, sp_helpindex)
- Added full Sybase-to-Fivetran type mapping table
- Added optional table filtering via 'tables' config field
- Updated load test documentation to reflect sybase_ase_load_test.py
- Updated key functions table to reflect current codebase
- Bumped to version 2.0

* Restore README content

* Address PR review feedback: isinstance for Decimal, parameterised queries, extract get_selected_tables helper

* Fix Sybase ASE cloud sync: bundle libtdsodbc.so directly since installation.sh is never executed by the platform, and hardcode schema() to avoid gRPC timeout from DB connection

* Address PR feedback: replace config placeholders with real values, add retry logic to pyodbc.connect()

* Update examples/private_preview_features/sybase_ase/connector.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR feedback: remove unused get_table_columns() function and configuration.json.example

* Fix Node.js 20 deprecation warnings: bump action versions to Node.js 24 compatible

* Fix flake8 W503: rewrite retry log line to avoid line break before binary operator

* Remove get_table_columns() from README key functions table

* Revert workflow action version bumps — out of scope for this PR

* Restructure README to match README_template.md structure

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: responsive multi-viewport capture suite

Capture screenshots at multiple named breakpoints in a single call with
optional pairwise pixel diffs between consecutive viewports.

**Types (packages/shared/src/browser.ts)**
- VIEWPORT_PRESETS const: 8 named presets from mobile-sm (375x667) to
  wide (1920x1080), each with width/height/mobile/label
- ViewportPresetName: keyof VIEWPORT_PRESETS
- ViewportPreset: { name, width, height, mobile, label }
- ViewportCapture: { preset, snapshotId, screenshot }
- ViewportSuiteReport: { tabId, runId, captures[], diffs[], capturedAt }

**TabManager (apps/desktop/src/main/tab-manager.ts)**
- captureViewportSuite(tabId, presets?, includeDiffs?)
  - Saves existing emulated viewport before run; restores after

    captureNamedScreenshot (snapshotId: tabId__preset__runId)
  - includeDiffs: pairwise diffScreenshots on consecutive captures
  - Restores original viewport or clears override if none was set

**AgentServer (apps/desktop/src/main/agent-server.ts)**
- POST /api/tabs/:id/viewport-suite { presets?, includeDiffs? }

**Agent SDK (packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts)**
 ViewportSuiteReport
- Exports: ViewportPresetName, ViewportSuiteReport, VIEWPORT_PRESETS

**Docs**
- docs/dev-team-features.md: updated section 9 with full API docs,
  preset reference table, and usage example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

example: set device cookie httpOnly (#868)

Fix for
[https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5](https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5)

To fix the problem in general, any cookie that stores authentication or
authorization-related data and is not intended to be accessed by
client-side JavaScript should be created with `HttpOnly: true`. In Go,
this is done by setting the `HttpOnly` field on the `http.Cookie`
struct.

In this specific file, the cookie is created in `loginHandler` at lines
164–169. We should update the `http.Cookie` literal to include
`HttpOnly: true`. This leaves the rest of the behavior unchanged: same
name, value, path, and use of `securecookie` for encoding. No other code
changes are required, and no new imports are needed, because `HttpOnly`
is already part of the `net/http` standard library’s `Cookie` struct.

Concretely:
- In `example/server/exampleop/device.go`, locate the cookie creation in
`loginHandler`.
- Modify the struct literal for `cookie` to add `HttpOnly: true`.
- Keep `Name`, `Value`, and `Path` as they are.

This single change will address all three CodeQL alert variants, since
they all refer to the same cookie.


_Suggested fixes powered by Copilot Autofix. Review carefully before
merging._

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Gayathri Vijayan <66356931+grvijayan@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial EFN MCP server implementation

MCP server for EFN (Ekonomikanalen) providing Swedish financial news,
expert analysis, and market commentary via 10 tools:

News: get_latest_news, get_article, get_breaking_news,
      search_news_by_topic, list_categories
Analysis: get_analyst_picks, get_market_commentary, get_report_commentary
Media: get_play_episodes, get_podcast_episodes

Built with FastMCP 3.0, Python 3.12, httpx, BeautifulSoup.
Includes 3 prompts, 2 resources, and 29 unit tests.

Designed to complement avanza-mcp (quantitative data) with
qualitative editorial content — zero overlap.

commit.message:
Pass WilsonVersion for IdentityModel external package build (#1629)

The IdentityModel repo's build/common.props computes FileVersion and
AssemblyVersion from WilsonVersion, not Version. Without passing
WilsonVersion, the build falls back to the hardcoded WilsonCurrentVersion
(8.0.0) instead of the release version (8.0.1), resulting in incorrect
file versions in the built DLLs.

Fix dotnet/source-build#5508

commit.message:
feat(email): sync daily quotas from provider APIs before sending

Replace in-memory-only counters with real usage data from each
provider's API at the start of every cascade run:
- Mailgun: GET /v3/{domain}/stats/total?event=accepted&duration=1d
- Mailjet: GET /v3/REST/statcounters (daily resolution)
- Unosend: GET /v1/emails (filter by today's date)
- Resend: GET /emails (filter by today's date)

This ensures accurate quota tracking across separate workflow runs
instead of relying on 429 responses to detect exhausted limits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Round 3 codebase cleanup — security fixes, endpoint dedup, frontend types

Phase 1: Security — redact redis_url from /cache/config, remove internal cache job endpoints, fix missing await in batch cancel
Phase 2: Remove duplicate batch endpoints from processing_routes (~45 lines)
Phase 3: Consolidate graph search — remove /search/entities, /search/frames, /search/advanced; enhance /search/hybrid with video_ids, entity_types, graph expansion toggle
Phase 4: Standardize all 17+ dual video_id OR patterns to single v.video_id across 6 files
Phase 5: Frontend type dedup — replace 7 duplicate Scene/Chapter/TranscriptSegment definitions with imports from @/types; remove debug console.logs; add null-safety for scene.duration
Phase 6: Frontend component audit — confirmed 'use client' removal has zero bundle benefit; deferred
Phase 7: Remove 5 dead endpoints (ffmpeg, ffmpeg/batch, 2x /search, jobs/submit); clean orphaned imports and dead test functions

30 files changed, +152/-1089 lines, 1184 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix release workflow verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ReboxLocal missing from bytecode merge/adjust and eval for-loop local visibility

Three locations scanned eval/module bytecode to skip operands but did not
account for ReboxLocal (opcode 109, 1-byte operand):

1. merge_eval_chunk in vm.rs — bytecode copier that adjusts constant
   indices and jump targets when merging eval code into the host chunk.
2. Non-configurable property check scanner in vm.rs — pre-scan of eval
   bytecode to detect illegal redefinitions.
3. adjust_merged_bytecode in opcode.rs — module bytecode IP/constant
   offset adjuster.

Missing ReboxLocal caused its 1-byte operand to be interpreted as the
next opcode, shifting all subsequent bytes and corrupting the entire
code stream. This manifested as panics (index out of bounds, unhandled
opcode 109) when eval or module code contained for-let loops.

Also extended top_level_locals_at_ip snapshots to cover all scope depths
at function_depth==0 (not just scope_depth==0), and added snapshot
recording at for-loop init entry/exit. This allows eval inside a
top-level for(let ...) body to see the loop variable.

Fixes: tagged-template/cache-eval-inner-function (cluster 15)
Fixes: module-code/namespace/define-own-property (clusters 10, 18)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
